mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost household disposable income and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment drop, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero within 6 months of a data revision.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized expansion in both manufacturing and construction sectors, confirming broad-based economic momentum that lifts corporate profit expectations and equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent annualized while core inflation remains below 2.5 percent creates a goldilocks environment that sustains corporate margin expansion without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, supporting positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns in the specified regime are statistically indistinguishable from zero (95% CI includes zero), or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which compresses corporate revenue guidance and triggers negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are positive in more than 60% of observations, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 45% over a 3-year period, or forward returns in tightening episodes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand that directly translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon when both series expand concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 50 basis points over a 2-year out-of-sample test, or the correlation between the proxy spread and forward returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts above 1.0 million annualized units signals strong consumer confidence and wealth effects that boost discretionary spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following housing starts above 1.0 million are negative in more than 55% of cases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when mortgage rates exceed 5.0%.
